windows.scorecard_note: unavailable: comparison input has no regime_segments

windows.regime_discrepancy_note: Binance dataset defaults all regime_id to 0 (TREND). Original MEXC paper trade used regime_id=2 (HIGH_VOL_CHAOTIC). Regime entry gates behaved differently in replay vs paper.
windows.scorecard_note: unavailable: comparison input has no regime_segments

findings.finding: No regime_segments in any window's comparison input — scorecards unavailable for both
findings.evidence: shadow_comparison.json files lack regime_segments field; CLI produced unavailable

findings.finding: #3028 regime discrepancy: replay uses regime_id=0 (TREND) but original paper used regime_id=2 (HIGH_VOL_CHAOTIC)
findings.impact: Drift classification confounded; regime entry/exits not comparable across replay vs paper

limitations: No regime_segments in shadow_comparison.json for either window
No step-level replay traces with regime_id per step
#3028 regime discrepancy is structural (Binance lacks regime data)
Narrow 1-2 minute windows unlikely to contain regime transitions
Single symbol (BTCUSDT) and single strategy (primary_breakout_v1) only
Proxy-only calibration — no explicit reject data for regime-level analysis
safety_boundaries: No runtime execution beyond committed artifacts
Binance candles are not MEXC evidence
Regime scorecards are diagnosis, not trade authorization
#3028 regime discrepancy is a structural confound, not a recoverable gap
No inference of regime behavior from aggregate metrics
